sprint: Magic Card Identity Evolution -- Phase 1: The Awakening
task: Implements the first slice of the Magic Card Identity Evolution design document -- a Magic Card never appears during onboarding or a settings menu, it awakens the moment a child completes their first Publish, and the child chooses whether to claim it. No sign-in/sign-up/email/password/account language anywhere -- the constellation IS the identity, claimed vs. unclaimed is the only distinction. Phase 1 is 100% local (no Supabase, no cross-device recall yet) -- claiming means only 'this device recognizes you.' New js/magicCard.js mints a claimed card's constellation pattern exactly once at claim time; a real bug (a separate throwaway pattern for the ceremony's own reveal, distinct from what claim() would persist) was found and fixed during implementation so the sky a child watches form is guaranteed to be the exact sky their card actually gets -- the design's own 'one constellation, two faces' promise, verified directly. New js/magicCardUI.js implements three screens sharing one overlay: the boot-time Identity Gate (shown only once a card has ever been claimed), the Awakening ceremony (triggered from Publish Studio's first-ever Publish, gated to fire at most once per browser), and Magic Card Home (reached via a new header glyph). Zero code changes were needed for the common zero-card case -- it lands on Creator's existing, unmodified Creation Flow Screen 1. Verified end-to-end via two new Playwright suites driving a real, complete first Publish through the actual UI: the zero-card boot path is unaffected, claiming persists a card whose sky matches the reveal exactly, the header badge and Magic Card Home work correctly, a second Publish never re-offers the ceremony, reboot with one claimed card shows the Returning Creator gate, a second claimed card shows the Shared Device picker, and Maybe Later/Just Exploring both defer without ever creating a card. Full regression across the existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors.
